message:
fix(lua,server): use exact float equality in Lua runtime, hoist timestamp in event loop

Lua runtime:
- Replace epsilon-based float comparison with exact equality (==) in
  lua_raw_equal, LuaTable::get, and LuaTable::set. Epsilon matching
  caused 1.0 and 1.0+EPSILON to alias as the same table key, violating
  Lua semantics. NaN==NaN now correctly returns false.
- Add tests verifying exact numeric key matching and NaN inequality.

Server event loop:
- Hoist the now_ms() call to once-per-poll-iteration and thread the
  timestamp through handle_readable → process_buffered_frames and
  check_blocked_clients, eliminating redundant syscalls and ensuring
  all commands in the same iteration see a consistent timestamp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
